headline: Air Pollution Linked to 12,800 Premature Deaths in Malaysia 2025
context: DOE and MOH joint study attributed 12,800 premature deaths to air quality exceeding WHO guidelines in 2025.

cards.big: 12,800 premature deaths attributed to air pollution in Malaysia during 2025
cards.sub: Transboundary haze and vehicle emissions are the primary contributors

cards.big: Malaysia's PM2.5 annual average of 21 micrograms exceeds WHO guideline of 5 micrograms
cards.sub: Population exposure to harmful air quality is 4 times above safe levels

cards.big: Clean Air Act proposed in 2019 has not been tabled in Parliament after 7 years
cards.sub: Legislative vacuum means no enforceable national air quality standards exist

cards.big: China's Clean Air Action Plan reduced PM2.5 by 40% in Beijing between 2013 and 2023
cards.sub: Strong regulatory action with industrial compliance produced rapid improvements

cards.big: Every breath of polluted air is a health cost that someone eventually pays—usually the poorest
Should Malaysia pass the Clean Air Act and set enforceable emission standards
